sourceTitle: Unearth fresh insights from your personal knowledge library using Q&A

questions.question: How do you open the Notion Q&A feature while working in a page?

options.text: Click the purple sparkle icon in the bottom‑right corner
options.explanation: The guide specifies that the purple sparkle in the bottom right opens Q&A.

options.text: Press Ctrl + Shift + Q
options.explanation: There is no keyboard shortcut mentioned for opening Q&A.

options.text: Select “Q&A” from the top‑right three‑dot menu
options.explanation: Q&A is accessed via the sparkle, not the three‑dot menu.

options.text: Type “/qa” in the page and press Enter
options.explanation: The slash command creates a block; it does not launch the Q&A assistant.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the data sources that Notion Q&A can draw information from?

options.text: Only the pages and databases inside your current workspace
options.explanation: Q&A is limited to content within your Notion workspace and does not access external data.

options.text: Any public web page you have bookmarked
options.explanation: Q&A does not search the web; it only uses internal pages.

options.text: All files attached to your Notion account, including PDFs
options.explanation: While attached files can be referenced if they are part of a page, Q&A still searches the page content, not the file contents directly.

options.text: Your personal email inbox linked to Notion
options.explanation: Email is not part of the workspace data that Q&A queries.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using Q&A over manually pruning your Notion archives?

options.text: You can keep everything and rely on Q&A to surface the most relevant ideas
options.explanation: The guide highlights that Q&A removes the need to delete content; it filters and synthesizes on demand.

options.text: Q&A automatically deletes duplicate pages
options.explanation: Q&A does not delete content; it only retrieves and summarizes.

options.text: It converts all notes into audio recordings
options.explanation: Audio conversion is not a feature of Q&A.

options.text: It syncs your Notion library with external cloud storage
options.explanation: Syncing with external storage is unrelated to Q&A's purpose.

questions.question: A student wants to know which year a particular textbook was published and who authored it. Which capability of Q&A makes this possible?

options.text: Q&A can search the student’s notes and return specific facts with citations
options.explanation: The guide states Q&A can answer specific questions about research, pulling from relevant pages and citing sources.

options.text: Q&A can query external library databases for bibliographic data
options.explanation: Q&A does not have access to external databases; it only uses internal workspace content.

options.text: Q&A generates a new summary page that lists all textbooks in the workspace
options.explanation: While it can synthesize information, it does not automatically create a list unless prompted.

options.text: Q&A can read PDF files attached to the notes and extract metadata
options.explanation: Metadata extraction from PDFs is not mentioned as a Q&A function.

questions.question: When using Q&A to develop a content strategy, which workflow does the guide recommend?

options.text: Ask Q&A to locate snippets of inspiration, then use AI Writer to draft the strategy
options.explanation: The guide suggests using Q&A to gather insights and then AI Writer for drafting.

options.text: Export all notes to a CSV file and manually sort them
options.explanation: Exporting to CSV is not part of the recommended Q&A workflow.

options.text: Create a new database and let Q&A auto‑populate it with content ideas
options.explanation: Q&A does not automatically create database entries; you must ask for the information.

options.text: Use Q&A to directly publish posts to social media platforms
options.explanation: Publishing is outside Q&A's scope; it only retrieves and synthesizes information.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the difference between Notion’s Writer and Q&A tools?

options.text: Writer can pull in external knowledge while Q&A only works with internal workspace content
options.explanation: Writer accesses external data for drafting; Q&A is confined to the workspace.

options.text: Writer automatically organizes your database properties, whereas Q&A edits page formatting
options.explanation: Neither tool manages database properties or page formatting directly.

options.text: Q&A generates long‑form articles, while Writer answers short factual questions
options.explanation: The opposite is true: Q&A answers questions, Writer drafts longer content.

options.text: Both tools have identical functionality; they are just named differently
options.explanation: The guide clearly distinguishes their functions.

questions.question: An entrepreneur wants to identify three prospects they haven’t contacted in the last month. How should they phrase the request to Q&A for optimal results?

options.text: Identify 3 prospects I haven’t contacted in the last month
options.explanation: The guide provides this exact phrasing as an example of a useful query.

options.text: Show me all contacts
options.explanation: This is too broad and won’t filter by date or number.

options.text: Delete any prospect not contacted in 30 days
options.explanation: Q&A does not perform deletions; it only retrieves information.

options.text: Export my CRM to Excel
options.explanation: Exporting is not a Q&A capability.

questions.question: Why might a user prefer Q&A over manually scanning pages when trying to discover conflicting research on a topic?

options.text: Q&A can automatically aggregate and present opposing viewpoints from multiple pages in a single answer
options.explanation: The guide mentions using Q&A to compile conflicting information, saving time and providing a synthesized view.

options.text: Q&A rewrites all sources into a uniform narrative, eliminating contradictions
options.explanation: Q&A does not erase contradictions; it surfaces them.

options.text: Q&A can access external academic databases to verify the credibility of each source
options.explanation: Q&A is limited to internal workspace content and cannot query external databases.

options.text: Q&A automatically tags each piece of conflicting research with a confidence score
options.explanation: Confidence scoring is not a described feature of Q&A.

questions.question: What is the role of citations in Q&A responses, and why are they important?

options.text: Citations show exactly which pages the answer was derived from, ensuring transparency and traceability
options.explanation: The guide emphasizes that Q&A always cites its sources, which helps users verify information.

options.text: Citations are placeholders that will be replaced by external URLs after publishing
options.explanation: Citations refer to internal Notion pages, not external URLs.

options.text: Citations allow Q&A to edit the original pages automatically
options.explanation: Q&A does not edit pages; it only reads and summarizes them.

options.text: Citations are optional and only appear when the user enables a special setting
options.explanation: Citations are always included; they are not optional.
